int findIntegers(int n) {
    string bin_n = convert_to_binary(n);
    int len = bin_n.length();
    vector<vector<vector<int>>> memo(len+1, vector<vector<int>>(2, vector<int>(2, -1)));
    
    function<int(int, int, bool)> dp = [&](int pos, int prev, bool tight) -> int {
        if (pos == len) return 1;
        if (memo[pos][prev][tight] != -1) return memo[pos][prev][tight];
        
        int limit = tight ? (bin_n[pos] - '0') : 1;
        int total = 0;
        
        for (int b = 0; b <= limit; ++b) {
            if (prev == 1 && b == 1) continue;
            bool new_tight = tight && (b == limit);
            total += dp(pos + 1, b, new_tight);
        }
        
        memo[pos][prev][tight] = total;
        return total;
    };
    
    return dp(0, 0, true);
}

The code in findIntegers:

#include <string>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int findIntegers(int n) {
        string bin_n;
        int original_n = n;
        while (n > 0) {
            bin_n = (n % 2 == 0 ? "0" : "1") + bin_n;
            n /= 2;
        }
        int len = bin_n.length();
        vector<vector<vector<int>>> memo(len + 1, vector<vector<int>>(2, vector<int>(2, -1)));
        
        auto dp = [&](int pos, int prev, bool tight) -> int {
            if (pos == len) return 1;
            if (memo[pos][prev][tight] != -1) return memo[pos][prev][tight];
            
            int limit = tight ? (bin_n[pos] - '0') : 1;
            int total = 0;
            
            for (int b = 0; b <= limit; ++b) {
                if (prev == 1 && b == 1) continue;
                bool new_tight = tight && (b == limit);
                total += dp(pos + 1, b, new_tight);
            }
            
            memo[pos][prev][tight] = total;
            return total;
        };
        
        return dp(0, 0, true);
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <string>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int findIntegers(int n) {
        string bin_n;
        int original_n = n;
        while (original_n > 0) {
            bin_n = (original_n % 2 == 0 ? "0" : "1") + bin_n;
            original_n /= 2;
        }
        int len = bin_n.length();
        vector<vector<vector<int>>> memo(len + 1, vector<vector<int>>(2, vector<int>(2, -1)));
        
        auto dp = [&](int pos, int prev, bool tight) -> int {
            if (pos == len) return 1;
            if (memo[pos][prev][tight] != -1) return memo[pos][prev][tight];
            
            int limit = tight ? (bin_n[pos] - '0') : 1;
            int total = 0;
            
            for (int b = 0; b <= limit; ++b) {
                if (prev == 1 && b == 1) continue;
                bool new_tight = tight && (b == limit);
                total += dp(pos + 1, b, new_tight);
            }
            
            memo[pos][prev][tight] = total;
            return total;
        };
        
        return dp(0, 0, true);
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(30) since the binary length is at most 30 bits.

space complexity: O(30 * 2 * 2) = O(120), which is constant space.

energy implications: LOW due to the efficient algorithm with minimal computations.

explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming with memoization to count valid binary numbers without consecutive ones up to n. It processes each bit position, tracking the previous bit and whether the current number is still tight to n's binary representation. This approach efficiently prunes invalid paths and ensures optimal performance.
